UT oday he will leave for Lewiston,
Idaho, where he intends to put in a
very strong union. Mr.. Williams was
granted seat of privilege at last
nights meeting of the trades council.


1t Is reported that at the next
meeting of the Retail Clerks union
active measures will be taken for the
purpose of organizing the lady clerks.
This movement will be encountered
with many difficulties. but when once
organized it is believed that the
union will be a potent factor in the
city.


The National Brewery Workers
union will hold special convention
in February to consider the question
OF jurisdiction over transfers, sta
tionary engineers, firemen and other
crafts employed in the brewery in
dustry.


Kansas City BARRIERS will present q
bill to the next legislature provid-
ing for the creation of board of
examination and inspection. BARRIERS
claim that this will be a protection
for the public as well as to the craft.


delegation from the American
Labor union called upon the city
council last night with a petition
asking that the city employ only
union labor The matter was referred
to the finance committee.
